title: Sticherus oceanicus
taxonomy: Sticherus oceanicus belongs to the family of plants known as Gleicheniaceae, which is a group of ferns. This plant is classified under the genus Sticherus, which contains several species of ferns found in tropical and subtropical regions. The Sticherus oceanicus is an evergreen, epiphytic fern that grows in the coastal regions of Australia. The classification of Sticherus oceanicus also places it within the division Pteridophyta, which is a group of vascular plants that reproduce via spores. The specific classification of Sticherus oceanicus reflects its unique characteristics and adaptations to its environment.
